stories.title: Sign in to Coder
stories.intro: Coder is where a software project lives: its vision, the repos it touches, its team and its user stories. Like every OpenApe app you sign in with your email domain and a passkey — no new account, no password.

steps.title: Open Coder
steps.caption: The start page is the sign-in: enter your email and continue.

steps.title: Signed in — your projects
steps.caption: On your first visit the list is empty. You only ever see projects you belong to — nobody outside a project can tell it, or its people, exist.

stories.title: Create a project
stories.intro: A project is the home for one software effort. You create it, become its admin, and give it a vision and the repos it touches — the shared context everyone on the team reads.

steps.title: Start a new project
steps.caption: Click **New project** and name it. As its creator you become the project admin.

steps.title: Name it
steps.caption: The project page opens: an overview with its vision and repos, a members panel, and the story board.

steps.title: Vision and repos
steps.caption: The vision is plain prose; the repos are full URLs to the code it touches — GitHub, GitLab, Forgejo or self-hosted, never just `owner/repo`. Both stay editable as the project grows.

stories.title: Add a user story
stories.intro: User stories live in the project, where everyone can see them — not in mail threads. A story has the usual parts and can carry repos, links, test references and a status.

steps.title: Open the story board
steps.caption: The board shows every story in the project at a glance. It is empty for a new project.

steps.title: Write the story
steps.caption: Give it a title and the story sentence — *As … I want … so that …*. The optional repos, links and test references can be added afterwards.

steps.title: Story created
steps.caption: The story opens, laid out for reading — sentence, criteria and any references — no files or code to open.

stories.title: Browse the board
stories.intro: Back on the board, every story shows its status. Group or filter to see exactly where the project stands.

steps.title: All stories with status
steps.caption: Each story carries a status badge. The board groups them so you read the project state in one look.

stories.title: Invite the team
stories.intro: A project admin adds people by email and controls, per member, what they may do. No email is sent — a new member is added read-only and sees it in their inbox at the next sign-in. Every write capability is granted explicitly.

steps.title: Open the members panel
steps.caption: The Members panel lists who belongs to the project and their role.

steps.title: Add by email
steps.caption: Add a person by email address. The app never reveals whether that address already has an OpenApe identity — and only a human admin, never an agent, can do it.

steps.title: They'll see it in their inbox
steps.caption: No email is sent: the new member is added as read-only and learns of it from an inbox notification the next time they sign in. The optional button drafts a heads-up in your own mail client — nothing leaves through our server.
